note: Sprint 5.27 Trinity Task Queue Runner v0.1: extends scripts/trinity/task_queue.py with a new run-batch subcommand that processes up to --max-items pending queue items oldest-first under strict local-only limits. Reuses the existing run_once() logic (Sprint 5.26) without duplication — a static safety test asserts _run_operator_loop and _run_watchdog each appear exactly twice in the file (definition + single call site inside run_once) so a copy-paste regression fails CI. Bounded by design and explicitly NOT a daemon: --max-items clamped 1..50, --sleep-seconds clamped 0..3600, both checked at entry to run_batch with named constants (RUNNER_MIN_BATCH, RUNNER_MAX_BATCH, RUNNER_MAX_SLEEP_SECONDS). Optional --stop-on-failure halts on the first failed item. Optional --report-path overrides the default location <queue-dir>/reports/_batches/TRINITY_TASK_QUEUE_RUNNER_REPORT_<batch_id>.json. New schema schemas/trinity/task_queue_runner_report.schema.json ($id=trinity-task-queue-runner-report/v0.1) with batch_id ^tqr-[0-9a-f]{16}$, item_ids arrays of ^qit-[0-9a-f]{16}$, safety_status enum {ok, warning, failed}, max_items 1..50, sleep_seconds 0..3600. Deterministic batch_id = sha16(pinned_time + queue_dir basename + max_items + stop_on_failure + item_ids attempted). safety_status precedence: ok (every attempted item completed) > warning (at least one failed but the runner kept going) > failed (at least one failed and --stop-on-failure was set, runner halted). Mode lock re-asserted in run_batch even though run_once already enforces it — belt-and-braces against a future caller fanning a non-local mode into the bounded path. Sibling scripts still invoked via argv subprocess only; no shell=True; no sibling-module imports of autonomy_governor / governor_watchdog / useful_compute_operator_loop. Inherits every fail-closed invariant from Sprint 5.26: operator_loop rc=3 (governor hard-block: halt_file_present or policy_mutated_at_runtime) marks the item failed; watchdog safety_status=critical marks the item failed even when operator_loop returned rc=0; any other non-zero rc marks the item failed. CLI rc=0 unless the runner itself can't start; rc=2 for missing queue / out-of-range bounds / unknown subcommand. Cross-check safety re-asserts that Sprint 5.27 did NOT regress Governor or Watchdog forbidden-token surfaces. Tests: 1125 passed, 37 skipped on the deployment host (1090 prior + 35 new = 25 functional + 10 safety). Manual demo: init queue, enqueue two scientific_intake fixtures with different pinned_times, run-batch --max-items 2 completes both in a single invocation with attempted_count=2 completed_count=2 failed_count=0 safety_status=ok. Non-goals for v0.1 (documented): no daemon, no concurrent batches (atomic queue.json write narrows race but no proper locking), no retry policy (failed items stay failed), no priority (strict FIFO by created_at), no webhook / external dispatch, no --fail-on critical exit-code mode (rc=0 even on safety_status=failed; rc=2 reserved for runner-can't-start), no wallet / signing / broadcast anywhere. Pure scripts+schemas+docs+tests merge: zero src/, zero consensus changes, zero wallet/payment/broadcast changes.
